name: Tex-Mex Tortilla Stack
recipeIngredient: 9 oz. package (2 cups) frozen chopped cooked chicken
6 oz. container frozen avocado dip, thawed
16 oz. can refried beans with green chili peppers or Mexican-style beans, drained and mashed
8 oz. carton reduced-fat or regular sour cream
1/2 cup finely chopped red sweet pepper
1 cup shredded lower-fat or regular cheddar cheese, or Monterey Jack cheese with jalapeno peppers
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium mixing bowl, combine the frozen chopped cooked chicken, jicama, and 1/2 cup taco sauce; set aside.
2. Place one of the flour tortillas on a platter. Spread with half of the chicken mixture.
3. Spread half of the avocado dip onto a second tortilla; place, avocado side up, atop the chicken.
4. Sprinkle with half of the lettuce.
5. Top with a third tortilla. Spread with half of the beans.
6. Add another tortilla. Place half each of the sour cream, red pepper, green onion, and cheese on top.
7. Repeat layers, ending with remaining sour cream, red pepper, green onion, and cheese.
8. Sprinkle with olives if desired.
9. Serve immediately or cover and chill for up to 3 hours.
10. Cut into wedges before serving. Pass taco sauce alongside.
Nutrition facts per serving: 392 calories, 15 grams total fat (3 grams saturated fat), 42 milligrams cholesterol, 566 milligrams sodium, 42 grams carbohydrates, 4 grams fiber, 24 grams protein. Daily values are based on a 2,000-calorie diet.
